title: Trump's battle for Greenland: Why the stock market hates it all
summary: The market hates surprises, usually.

title: Dow tumbles more than 850 points and dollar slides over Greenland and tariff threats
summary: Investors revived the “Sell America” trade Tuesday and sold off US stocks, bonds and the dollar amid concerns about President Donald Trump’s clash with European leaders over ownership of Greenland.

title: Treasuries, Stocks Sell Off as Greenland and Japan Shatter Calm
summary: As stock markets opened on Tuesday, the “Sell America” trade came back in full force as Treasuries tumbled, driving up yields, and the dollar slid.  The moves show that investors’ previous willingness to shrug off Trump’s actions — including the White House’s capture of Venezuela’s leader, its threats to nearby countries, and renewed attacks on the Federal Reserve — is beginning to erode.  Trump’s demand for US control over Greenland is stoking investor anxieties about potential worst-case scenarios, including a rupture in the NATO alliance, a full-blown trade war, or European steps to foment market turbulence as a way to force the Trump administration to back down.

title: Volatility is Back, and It's Weighing on Bitcoin. Is Crypto a Hedge or a Risk Asset This Time?
summary: Volatility has soared after President Donald Trump's latest comments suggested the U.S. might take Greenland by force. As investors panned risk assets, the price of bitcoin also took a hit.

title: Wall Street’s ‘Fear Gauge’ Jumps the Most Since October
summary: The Cboe Volatility Index, a measure of expected stock volatility, jumped more than 20% Tuesday, on pace for its biggest one-day rise since October. The Vix typically surges during stock selloffs. It leapt 32% on Oct.

title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures rise after Trump's Greenland threats prompt worst day since October
summary: Stock futures have risen following the worst day in months as Trump reignites tariff talk with EU members.

title: Stock market today: Dow plummets 800 points, S&P 500, Nasdaq sink over 2% as Trump's Greenland threats clobber stocks
summary: The market is on edge over Trump's threatened tariffs over Greenland and a Japan-led global bond sell-off.

title: Gold Likely to Be Supported by ‘Price-Insensitive’ Central Banks
summary: Gold is likely to have “enduring support” from sustained, largely price-insensitive demand from central banks looking to diversify their reserves, said T. Rowe Price’s Matt Bance.

title: US futures edge higher and gold hits another record as markets swoon over Greenland dispute
summary: Asian shares mostly extended their losses on Wednesday and gold prices hit a record high as concerns over U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff threats on Greenland fueled unease among investors.  U.S. futures rose after steep losses Tuesday on Wall Street.  Gold prices crossed the $4,800 mark for the first time, gaining 1.7% as money flowed into assets considered to be safe havens at times of uncertainty.

title: Dow Jones Futures Rise After Trump's Greenland Threats Trigger Sell-Off; Tesla, Nvidia Skid
summary: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq broke key support on Trump's Greenland push with his Davos speech on tap. Nvidia, Tesla and other megacaps slumped. Netflix fell late.

title: Treasury Yields Hit Highest Levels Since Last Summer
summary: U.S. Treasurys are under pressure from multiple fronts, with investors reacting to President Trump’s bid to take Greenland and new signs that Japan could pursue expansionary fiscal policies.  Falling bond prices pushed the yield on the 30-year bond up around 0.08 percentage point to 4.92%.  The rise in yields was led by those on longer-term Treasurys, resembling previous episodes when investors have worried about the fallout from President Trump’s aggressive tariff policies.

title: Nasdaq Turns Negative for the Year. Sentiment Looks 'Morose.'
summary: A 'Sell America' theme is emerging as the Greenland dispute escalates, with Treasuries and the dollar losing favor and the Nasdaq approaching a year-to-date loss.  The Dow was down about 900 points or 1.8%, while the S&P 500 was down 2%.  The tech-heavy Nasdaq, down 2.3%, is now trading at 22,981, putting it on track to be negative for the year.

title: Danish Pension Fund Is Exiting Treasuries—but Will Stick With U.S. Assets
summary: AkademikerPension’s chief investment officer said the move was driven by the U.S. government’s poor finances, but that Trump’s action didn’t make the call more difficult.

title: Higher Treasury Yields Weigh on Home Builder Stocks
summary: A higher 10-year Treasury yield means higher mortgage rates.  The iShares U.S. Home Construction ETF was 1.5% lower in mid-morning trading, on pace for its largest percentage decline since Jan. 7, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Builder stocks had been riding a wave of optimism spurred by a decline in mortgage rates earlier this month.

title: Have Trump’s Greenland Threats Revived the ‘Sell America’ Trade?
summary: The dollar and U.S. stocks are sliding, while longer-dated Treasury yields are jumping. Sound familiar? President Trump's Greenland threats have renewed fears of a trade war with Europe, sparking some of the same moves that sent markets haywire after the White House's "Liberation Day" tariffs in April.

title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures rise after Trump's Greenland threats prompt worst day since October
summary: Stock futures have risen following the worst day in months as Trump reignites tariff talk with EU members.

title: Gold and silver surge to new highs on tariff threats, Greenland
summary: Gold (GC=F) and silver prices (SI=F) surged to new record highs on Tuesday following President Trump's latest tariff threats against European nations that don't support the proposed US acquisition of Greenland. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré outlines the geopolitical headlines driving commodities prices higher. title: Gold, silver rise on Greenland drama
summary: Gold (GC=F) and silver (SI=F) futures are jumping on Tuesday as President Trump reignited trade-war tensions with Europe over Greenland. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Ines Ferré reports the latest.  To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Opening Bid.

title: Gold Extends Powerful Rally as Crisis Over Greenland Worsens
summary: President Donald Trump, who is scheduled to address the World Economic Forum at Davos in Switzerland, showed no signs of backing down on his grab for the Arctic island.  Copper joined the metals surge with gains toward $13,000 a ton, as Goldman Sachs Group Inc. forecast continued flows into the US — a key driver behind the industrial metal’s powerful price rally.
